summarization.a: The figure displays a Mo 3d spectra chart with peaks at specific binding energies, after etching with one 4 s NbCl5 pulse

data_extraction.a:
| Oxidation State | Peak Assignment | Binding Energy (eV) |
|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------------|
| Mo⁰            | Mo 3d          | ~228                |
| Mo³⁺           | Mo 3d          | ~230                |
| Mo⁴⁺           | Mo 3d          | ~231                |
| Mo⁵⁺           | Mo 3d          | ~232                |
| Mo⁶⁺           | Mo 3d          | ~234                |
| Additional Mo⁶⁺ component  | -      | ~236                |

a.question: How do the Mo intensities compare to the unetched film?
a.answer: After the first NbCl5 pulse, a decrease in the Mo6+ and Mo5+ intensities is seen compared to the unetched film whereas Mo4+ has higher relative intensity than before etching

a.question: How does the metallic intensities predict formation of MoCl3?
a.answer: The intensity of the metallic Mo0 is also higher, which indicates etching (thinning) of the surface oxide. An increase of the intensity between the oxide and metal peaks is seen, which corresponds to peaks at 229.6 and 232.7 eV. These are attributed to Mo3+, likely in the form of MoCl3.

a.question: How can the MoCl3 generation be verified?
a.answer: The generation of MoCl3 is supported by the thermodynamic equilibrium calculations, which indicated that NbCl5 can react with Mo to make lower valence chlorides.
